_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 = 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This headline rise reflects significant price changes concentrated in certain commodities rather than a uniform increase. The climb has been uneven, with some items experiencing much sharper gains than others. Overall, the data highlight a notable upward move in this group of commonly consumed soft commodities.
notes.movers: Among the basket components, coffee surged by 99% since 2015, the largest increase, followed by orange juice at +34%, wheat +19%, and sugar +13%. These price moves are driven primarily by weather events and crop diseases such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee regions and citrus greening plus hurricanes impacting orange juice supply. Because soft commodity markets are relatively thin—meaning they have less volume and liquidity—such disruptions can sharply move prices rather than broad economic inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is predominantly due to a handful of weather- and disease-sensitive crops. Given the vulnerability of these soft commodities to environmental factors, elevated price levels may persist for years as supply adjusts and recovery efforts take place. Market participants should view these price changes as driven by supply-side shocks rather than systemic inflationary pressures.
